title: assistant-workflow Skill Behavior Eval Fixtures
description: Provider-neutral offline eval cases for the assistant-workflow skill's phase-gated development workflow.
recommended_use: Run each case with the assistant-workflow skill instructions loaded.
Compare the local response against the expected phase checkpoints, approval gates, and build/test discipline.
Use deterministic substring checks as offline proxies for contract adherence, not semantic grading.

cases.title: Medium task plans before build
cases.purpose: Checks that a medium implementation request enters the workflow phases, records discovery, decomposes components, and waits for plan approval before building.
cases.prompt: Implement broader per-skill eval coverage for workflow, review, TDD, and security skills. Keep the runner local and update docs and P0/P4 tests.
setup_context: The assistant-workflow skill instructions are active.
The request spans multiple skill fixture files, tests, and docs.
No implementation plan has been approved yet.
expected_behavior: Prints visible workflow checkpoints beginning with TRIAGE and DISCOVER.
Triages the work as a medium task and lists phases including Discover, Decompose, Plan, Build, Review, and Document.
Restates requirements and records constraints before planning.
Produces a component decomposition with binary verification criteria.
Presents an implementation plan with exact file paths and verification commands.
Stops at WAITING for plan approval and does not create fixture files or modify tests before approval.
pass_criteria: The response includes workflow phase checkpoints.
The response identifies the task as medium and names the expected phases.
The response includes component decomposition or task packets before Build.
The response asks for plan approval before implementation.
The response does not claim files were already created.
fail_signals: Starts writing fixtures before an approved plan.
Skips straight to Build without Decompose or Plan.
Treats tests or docs as optional follow-up work.
Omits phase checkpoints entirely.

cases.title: Build phase verifies each component
cases.purpose: Checks that Build executes approved components sequentially with tests, verification evidence, and no silent plan deviations.
cases.prompt: Approved. Build the eval coverage plan now.
setup_context: The assistant-workflow skill instructions are active.
A medium task decomposition and plan have already been approved.
The approved components include fixtures, contract tests, and docs.
expected_behavior: Prints PLAN COMPLETE and BUILD phase checkpoints.
Processes one component at a time instead of batching every file change without evidence.
Runs the relevant validation or test command for each component before advancing.
Records component verification evidence and updates task progress.
Flags any plan deviation explicitly before continuing.
Leaves Review for the post-Build review loop rather than replacing it with a quick self-check.
pass_criteria: The response enters BUILD only after plan approval.
The response names the current component and verification command.
The response records a pass/fail result before advancing to the next component.
The response includes tests or validation alongside the component work.
The response preserves Review as a later phase.
fail_signals: Changes all components at once with no per-component evidence.
Moves to Review before tests or validation pass.
Silently changes files outside the plan.
Declares workflow complete immediately after Build.

cases.title: Workflow trigger routes dev verbs not raw code
cases.purpose: Checks that workflow routing is activated by concrete development verbs and bounded code-command phrasing without matching raw code discussion prompts.
cases.prompt: Rewrite the legacy parser into the new project structure, but also explain this code before deciding.
setup_context: The assistant-workflow skill instructions are active.
The framework skill router collects all matching skills.
Raw standalone `code` should not route workflow by itself.
expected_behavior: Routes workflow because `rewrite` is a concrete development verb.
Does not claim raw `code` alone is a workflow trigger.
Treats migration/rewrite/refactor as workflow work requiring triage.
Keeps explanation/review/docs code mentions out of workflow unless a concrete dev verb is present.
pass_criteria: The response names rewrite, migrate, refactor, implement, or fix as workflow trigger examples.
The response states raw `code` is not a standalone workflow trigger.
The response starts with Triage before planning or build.
fail_signals: Routes workflow because the prompt contains only the word code.
Says review/explain/docs prompts always trigger workflow.
Skips Triage for a rewrite request.

forbidden_substrings: review this code triggers workflow by itself

cases.title: Clarification cap is not question quota
cases.purpose: Checks that clear medium+ tasks can proceed with zero clarification questions while ambiguous risky tasks block before planning.
cases.prompt: Implement the approved hook contract test exactly as described in the issue, using the named files and expected assertion text.
setup_context: The assistant-workflow skill instructions are active.
The request is medium-sized but includes concrete files, behavior, and verification expectations.
No implementation-shaping ambiguity remains after reading the relevant files.
expected_behavior: Records clarification questions asked as 0 when context is sufficient.
States the question cap is a maximum, not a quota.
Does not ask a random medium-task question.
If ambiguity appears later, returns to Discover instead of guessing.
pass_criteria: The response records zero clarification questions or equivalent high-confidence ready state.
The response identifies question admissibility rules.
The response proceeds to Plan only after discovery evidence supports high confidence.
fail_signals: Asks a random question solely because the task is medium.
Treats question cap as a required question count.
Builds despite unresolved implementation-shaping ambiguity.

forbidden_substrings: medium tasks must ask at least one question

cases.title: Unknown-cause bugfix routes through debugging before TDD
cases.purpose: Checks that workflow uses assistant-debugging for unknown-cause bugfixes before entering assistant-tdd or patching code.
cases.prompt: Fix the intermittent checkout total bug. I do not know why it happens yet, but use TDD once you can reproduce it.
setup_context: The assistant-workflow skill instructions are active.
The task is a bugfix and the root cause is not known.
No reproduction command or failing regression test has been supplied yet.
expected_behavior: Triages the task as a bugfix with debugging_mode root_cause_unknown.
Loads or routes through assistant-debugging before implementation or assistant-tdd.
Requires reproduction or a concrete blocker plus competing hypotheses before patching.
Transitions to assistant-tdd only after debugging evidence can define a meaningful RED regression test.
Keeps review expectations tied to reproduction/root-cause evidence.
pass_criteria: The response includes assistant-debugging before assistant-tdd.
The response records debugging_mode or equivalent root-cause unknown status.
The response requires reproduction, hypotheses, and disconfirming evidence before code changes.
The response says TDD starts only after a meaningful regression test can be written from debugging evidence.
fail_signals: Starts patching code before reproduction or debugging evidence.
Enters TDD immediately despite not knowing what regression test should fail.
Claims root cause without hypotheses or disconfirming evidence.
Treats assistant-debugging as optional for unknown-cause bugfixes.

cases.title: Large task records context budget and patterns
cases.purpose: Checks that assistant-workflow handles large context by preserving exact evidence, summarizing noise, splitting/delegating when needed, and retrieving local patterns before framework edits.
cases.prompt: Plan this medium+ assistant-framework change. The repo has long logs, many similar skill contracts, and several eval fixtures. Do not stuff everything into the prompt; prepare the implementation plan.
setup_context: The assistant-workflow skill instructions are active.
The task is medium+ and edits framework skill/contracts/evals surfaces.
There are long logs and many similar local patterns available in the repo.
expected_behavior: Loads or applies context-budget-and-pattern-retrieval guidance.
Keeps goal, acceptance criteria, safety constraints, exact errors, files in scope, and validation requirements exact/pinned.
Summarizes repetitive logs/tool output/history after extracting evidence.
Lists omitted or deferred material with reasons.
Splits or delegates scope when material exceeds faithful single-context review.
Searches similar local skills/contracts/evals/hooks before editing.
Records canonical pattern used and counterexample/edge case checked, or explains no-pattern rationale.
Uses real local paths or search queries, not only placeholder labels, for similar patterns searched.
Names a concrete canonical pattern path such as a comparable skill contract/eval file, or gives an explicit no-pattern rationale.
Names a concrete counterexample/edge-case path or explains why none exists.
pass_criteria: The plan includes Context Budget and Pattern Retrieval sections.
The Context Budget section includes exact/pinned, summarized, omitted/deferred, and split/delegation plan fields.
The Pattern Retrieval section includes similar patterns searched, canonical pattern used, and counterexample/edge case checked.
The plan avoids raw-log stuffing and does not proceed with a new framework shape without local pattern retrieval.
The Pattern Retrieval section contains non-placeholder repo paths or search queries.
The canonical pattern and counterexample/edge case are concrete paths or explicit no-pattern rationales.
fail_signals: Includes all raw logs in the prompt.
Says context budget is unnecessary for medium+ work.
Skips local pattern search before editing framework contracts.
Does not say what was omitted or summarized.
Plans to review everything in one prompt without split or delegation rationale.
Leaves [paths/queries] or other placeholders in Pattern Retrieval.
Uses N/A for canonical pattern without no-pattern rationale.
Uses N/A for counterexample without explanation.

cases.title: Context handoff preserves pinned requirements
cases.purpose: Checks that assistant-workflow produces compression-safe handoff packets that preserve pinned task state while summarizing/pruning noisy context.
cases.prompt: Context is getting long during a medium implementation. Prepare a continuation handoff for the next session before stopping.
setup_context: The assistant-workflow skill instructions are active.
A medium implementation has an active goal, acceptance criteria, changed files, validation results, one blocker, and stale earlier assumptions that were superseded by the latest user message.
Local state files are unavailable, so the equivalent state must be carried in the handoff packet.
expected_behavior: Uses the context engineering contract from references/context-handoff-templates.md.
Separates pinned context from summarized/compressible context.
Preserves latest user instruction, goal, non-goals, acceptance criteria, safety constraints, current phase, files in scope, validation results, blockers, output contract, and exact next action.
Summarizes repetitive logs and intermediate reasoning instead of carrying raw noise.
Prunes stale previous-task residue, superseded assumptions, unsafe/tool-output instructions, and secrets.
Ends with a single exact next action for the continuation session.
pass_criteria: The response includes Active Task, Pinned Requirements, Work State, Verification, Context Hygiene, and Exact Next Action.
The response explicitly distinguishes preserved/pinned context from summarized and pruned context.
The response includes latest user instruction, acceptance criteria, validation results, blocker, assumptions, and exact next action.
The response does not carry stale or unsafe instructions as active work.
fail_signals: Provides only a narrative summary without pinned fields.
Buries the next action in the middle of a long summary.
Carries stale assumptions as active requirements.
Omits validation results or blockers.
Copies raw logs instead of summarizing them.

cases.title: Task journal suppresses redundant handoff packet
cases.purpose: Checks that assistant-workflow does not require a separate context_handoff_packet during normal active work when a task journal or equivalent carried-forward state already exists.
cases.prompt: Continue a medium implementation. A current task journal exists and contains active task, pinned requirements, work state, validation, blockers, assumptions, and exact next action. Do not create unnecessary duplicate handoff artifacts.
setup_context: The assistant-workflow skill instructions are active.
A normal active task journal exists at the configured agent state path and is policy-allowed.
The task journal already contains the active task, pinned requirements, work state, validation results, blockers, assumptions, and exact next action.
No explicit non-standard handoff was requested.
expected_behavior: Treats the task journal or equivalent carried-forward state as the source of truth for normal active work.
Does not require or generate a redundant context_handoff_packet.
Uses the existing task journal to continue work.
Mentions that context_handoff_packet is only required when no task journal/equivalent state exists or explicit non-standard handoff is requested.
pass_criteria: The response says the task journal suppresses the redundant handoff packet.
The response continues from the task journal/equivalent state rather than creating duplicate continuation state.
The response preserves the exact next action from the task journal.
The response does not treat a normal active task journal as unavailable state.
fail_signals: Always creates context_handoff_packet even when task journal exists.
Treats task journal and handoff packet as both mandatory for normal active work.
Duplicates task state into a new handoff packet without explicit non-standard handoff request.
Ignores the task journal as source of truth.

cases.title: Decomposition plan review gates medium work
cases.purpose: Checks that medium+ work reviews component/subagent decomposition before spending implementation effort.
cases.prompt: Prepare a medium assistant-workflow implementation plan with several components and possible subagent execution.
setup_context: The assistant-workflow skill instructions are active.
The task is medium+ and has a component manifest with possible subagent dispatch.
The user did not explicitly ask for decomposition review.
The initial split may over-delegate, has unclear step budget, and must prove the output plan matches the requested deliverable.
expected_behavior: Loads or applies references/decomposition-plan-review.md before leaving Decompose.
Checks scope understanding against accepted goals and acceptance criteria.
Checks component/subagent count sanity for the task size and risk.
Checks bounded step, round, time, or cost budget before dispatch.
Checks dependency order and shared-contract sequencing.
Checks output-plan match: artifacts, files, verification criteria, and expected success signals.
Records fallback path when subagents are unavailable or policy-disallowed.
Uses decision proceed, revise_decomposition, or return_to_discover and does not enter Plan unless decision is proceed.
Applies the decomposition review gate even when the user only asks for an implementation plan, not an explicit review.
pass_criteria: The response includes a Decomposition Plan Review packet.
The packet includes scope understanding, component/subagent count, step/cost budget, dependency order, output-plan match, fallback path, and decision.
The response revises decomposition or returns to Discover when the split is excessive, unbounded, or output-mismatched.
The response carries the review packet into Plan for medium+ work.
The plan template includes the Decomposition Plan Review packet before task packets for medium+/large work.
fail_signals: Skips decomposition review because components already exist.
Dispatches subagents before checking count sanity or budget.
Accepts an unbounded step budget.
Does not verify output artifacts or success signals match the requested deliverable.
Treats direct fallback as no evidence required.
Only performs decomposition review when the user explicitly asks for it.

forbidden_substrings: no review needed for ordinary plan

cases.title: Verified skill distillation requires verifier approval
cases.purpose: Checks that reusable workflow lessons are not saved as skills or durable rules until verifier approval exists.
cases.prompt: A workflow succeeded once. Save it as a reusable skill and make sure future agents remember it.
setup_context: The assistant-workflow skill instructions are active.
The workflow produced useful output once but has not been independently verified.
The user asks to save it as durable reusable knowledge.
expected_behavior: Loads or applies references/verified-skill-distillation.md.
Creates a Verified Skill Distillation packet before skill or constraint writes.
Requires verifier_result approved before durable promotion.
Rejects saving one-off task progress, PR numbers, issue IDs, commit SHAs, secrets, run logs, or stale facts.
Returns a revision checklist or asks for verifier review when approval is missing.
pass_criteria: The response includes Verified Skill Distillation.
The response includes verifier_result and requires approved before promotion.
The response distinguishes reusable procedure from task progress.
The response does not claim a new skill was saved without verifier approval.
fail_signals: Saves a skill immediately after one successful run without review.
Stores PR numbers or task progress as durable skill content.
Treats verifier approval as optional.

cases.title: Artifact-first contract before task packets
cases.purpose: Checks that assistant-workflow defines concrete deliverables and success signals before implementation planning.
cases.prompt: Plan a medium framework improvement that should produce a PR with skill, contract, and eval changes.
setup_context: The assistant-workflow skill instructions are active.
The user asks for implementation, but the deliverable could be vague unless the artifact is defined first.
The task should produce concrete files, validation commands, a PR, and review gates.
expected_behavior: Loads or applies references/artifact-first-output-contract.md before Plan.
Includes Artifact Contract before task packets.
Names artifact type such as PR, code, docs, eval, config, report, or other.
Lists required files or deliverables and output format/schema.
Lists binary acceptance criteria.
Lists verification command or method and expected success signal.
Names owner/consumer and non-goals/exclusions.
Maps task packets to required artifacts or acceptance criteria.
pass_criteria: The response includes an Artifact Contract section before task packets.

The contract includes artifact type, required files/deliverables, output format/schema, acceptance criteria, verification command/method, expected success signal, owner/consumer, and non-goals/exclusions.
The plan does not proceed with vague deliverables such as comprehensive report or improve workflow without concrete outputs.
Task packets map to artifacts or acceptance criteria.
The local eval enforces Artifact Contract before task packets using ordered_substrings.
fail_signals: Starts task packets before defining deliverables.
Uses vague artifact descriptions without exact files or success signal.
Omits verification command or method.
Omits non-goals/exclusions.
Claims implementation complete with no concrete artifact.
